Package org.aavso.tools.vstar.vela

Class VeLaInterpreter

java.lang.Object
org.aavso.tools.vstar.vela.VeLaInterpreter
public class VeLaInterpreter extends Object
VeLa: VStar expression Language interpreter

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    VeLaInterpreter()
    Construct a VeLa interpreter with verbose mode set to false, adding the VeLa API, and no source directories.
    VeLaInterpreter(boolean verbose)
    Construct a VeLa interpreter with verbose mode as specified, adding the VeLa API, and no source directories.
    VeLaInterpreter(boolean verbose, boolean addVStarAPI, List<File> sourceDirectories)
    Construct a VeLa interpreter with an initial scope and intrinsic functions.

    Given a class, add non zero-arity VeLa type-compatible functions to the functions map.

  • Constructor Details

    • VeLaInterpreter

      public VeLaInterpreter(boolean verbose, boolean addVStarAPI, List<File> sourceDirectories)
      Construct a VeLa interpreter with an initial scope and intrinsic functions.
      Parameters:
      verbose - Verbose mode?
      addVStarAPI - Add the VStar API?
      sourceDirectories - A list of source directories containing VeLa source files (ending in ".vl" or ".vela") to be loaded.

    • VeLaInterpreter

      public VeLaInterpreter(boolean verbose)
      Construct a VeLa interpreter with verbose mode as specified, adding the VeLa API, and no source directories.
      Parameters:
      verbose - Verbose mode?

    • VeLaInterpreter

      public VeLaInterpreter()
      Construct a VeLa interpreter with verbose mode set to false, adding the VeLa API, and no source directories.

  • Method Details

    • setVerbose

      public void setVerbose(boolean verbose)
      Parameters:
      verbose - the verbose to set

    • pushEnvironment

      public void pushEnvironment(VeLaEnvironment<Operand> environment)
      Push an environment onto the stack.
      Parameters:
      environment - The environment to be pushed.

    • popEnvironment

      public VeLaEnvironment<Operand> popEnvironment()
      Pop the top-most environment from the stack and return it.
      Returns:
      The top-most environment.

    • peekEnvironment

      public VeLaEnvironment<Operand> peekEnvironment()
      Peek the top-most environment on the stack and return it.
      Returns:
      The top-most environment.

    • getEnvironments

      public Stack<VeLaEnvironment<Operand>> getEnvironments()
      Returns:
      The stack of environments

    • getScopes

      public List<VeLaScope> getScopes()
      Return all scopes (activation records) on the stack as a list in order from oldest to newest.

    • getStack

      public Stack<Operand> getStack()
      Returns:
      the operand stack

    • pop

      public Operand pop(String msgOnError) throws VeLaEvalError
      Pop and return an operand from the stack if not empty.
      Parameters:
      msgOnError - The exception message to use if the stack is empty.
      Returns:
      The operand on top of the stack.
      Throws:
      VeLaEvalError - Thrown when the stack is empty.

    • program

      public Optional<Operand> program(File path) throws VeLaParseError, VeLaEvalError
      VeLa program interpreter entry point.
      Parameters:
      file - A path to a file containing a VeLa program string to be interpreted.
      Returns:
      An optional result, depending upon whether a value was left on the stack.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • program

      public Optional<Operand> program(String prog) throws VeLaParseError, VeLaEvalError
      VeLa program interpreter entry point.
      Parameters:
      prog - The VeLa program string to be interpreted.
      Returns:
      An optional result, depending upon whether a value was left on the stack.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • veLaToResultASTPair

      public Pair<Optional<Operand>,AST> veLaToResultASTPair(String prog) throws VeLaParseError, VeLaEvalError
      VeLa program interpreter entry point.
      Parameters:
      prog - The VeLa program string to be interpreted.
      Returns:
      A pair consisting of an optional result, depending upon whether a value was left on the stack, and the AST that gave rise to the result.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • expressionToOperand

      public Operand expressionToOperand(String expr) throws VeLaParseError, VeLaEvalError
      Expression interpreter entry point.
      Parameters:
      expr - The expression string to be interpreted.
      Returns:
      An operand.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • booleanExpression

      public boolean booleanExpression(String expr) throws VeLaParseError, VeLaEvalError
      Deprecated.
      Only used in test code: remove!
      VeLa boolean expression interpreter entry point.
      Parameters:
      expr - The VeLa expression string to be interpreted.
      Returns:
      A Boolean value result.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • commonInterpreter

      public Pair<Optional<Operand>,AST> commonInterpreter(String prog, org.antlr.v4.runtime.ParserRuleContext tree) throws VeLaEvalError
      Common VeLa evaluation entry point. This will be most effective when prog is an often used expression.
      Parameters:
      prog - The VeLa program string to be interpreted.
      tree - The result of parsing the VeLa expression.
      Returns:
      An optional result depending upon whether a value is left on the stack and the AST that was constructed and evaluated.
      Throws:
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • commonParseTreeWalker

      protected AST commonParseTreeWalker(String prog, org.antlr.v4.runtime.ParserRuleContext tree) throws VeLaParseError
      Common parse tree walker and AST generator.
      Parameters:
      prog - The VeLa program to be interpreted.
      tree - The parse tree resulting from parsing the VeLa expression.
      Returns:
      The abstract syntax tree created by walking the parse tree.
      Throws:
      VeLaParseError - If a parse error occurs.

    • eval

      public void eval(AST ast) throws VeLaEvalError

      Given an AST representing a VeLa program, interpret this via a depth first traversal, leaving the result of evaluation on the stack.

      The name "eval" is used in deference to John McCarthy's Lisp and its eval function, the equivalent of Maxwell's equations in Computer Science.

      I've also just noticed that VeLa is an anagram of eval! :)

      Parameters:
      ast - An abstract syntax tree.
      Throws:
      VeLaEvalError - If an evaluation error occurs.

    • bind

      public void bind(String name, Operand value, boolean isConstant)
      Given a variable name, search for it in the stack of environments, binding a value if found and the type of the binding is compatible with the type of the new value. The search proceeds from the top to the bottom of the stack, maintaining the natural stack ordering. If the name is not found, a new binding is created in the top-most scope.
      Parameters:
      name - The name to which to bind the value.
      value - The value to be bound.
      isConstant - Is this a constant binding?

    • lookupBinding

      public Optional<Operand> lookupBinding(String name)
      Given a variable name, search for it in the stack of environments, return an optional Operand instance. The search proceeds from the top to the bottom of the stack, maintaining the natural stack ordering.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the variable to look up.
      Returns:
      The optional operand.

    • lookupFunctions

      public Optional<List<FunctionExecutor>> lookupFunctions(String name)
      Given a function name, search for it in the stack of environments, return an optional list of function executors. The search proceeds from the top to the bottom of the stack, maintaining the natural stack ordering.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the variable to look up.
      Returns:
      The optional function executor list.

    • addFunctionExecutor

      public void addFunctionExecutor(FunctionExecutor executor)
      Add a function executor to the current scope.
      Parameters:
      executor - The function executor to be added.

    • addFunctionExecutorsFromClass

      public void addFunctionExecutorsFromClass(Class<?> clazz, Object instance, Set<Class<?>> permittedTypes, Set<String> exclusions)
      Given a class, add non zero-arity VeLa type-compatible functions to the functions map.
      Parameters:
      clazz - The class from which to add function executors.
      instance - The instance of this class on which to invoke the function.
      permittedTypes - The set of Java types that are compatible with VeLa.
      exclusions - Names of functions to exclude.
